title: Wounded Knee Massacre Memorial and Sacred Site Act
short_title: Wounded Knee Massacre Memorial and Sacred Site Act
stage_label: Committee on Indian Affairs. Reported by Senator Murkowski without amendment. With written report No. 119-72.
plain_summary: This law requires the Secretary of the Interior to give about 40 acres of land at the Wounded Knee Massacre site in South Dakota to two Native American tribes within one year. The land will become a protected memorial and sacred site that belongs to the Oglala Sioux Tribe and Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe together. The tribes will own the land under special rules that prevent them from selling it and protect it from state and local taxes. The land can only be used for purposes that both tribes agree on, and no gambling or other commercial businesses are allowed there.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Wounded Knee Massacre Memorial and Sacred Site Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Definitions
sections.text: In this law the following terms mean specific things. Restricted fee status means a special way of owning land where the Tribes will continue to own the land. The land will be part of the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and under the control of the Oglala Sioux Tribe for legal matters. The land cannot be sold or transferred without permission from Congress and the Tribes. State or local governments cannot tax this land. The Secretary of the Interior does not need to approve how the Tribes use this land as long as they follow an agreement between the Oglala Sioux Tribe and Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe dated October twenty first two thousand twenty two. Secretary means the Secretary of the Interior. Tribal land means about forty acres of land on the Pine Ridge Indian Reservation in Oglala Lakota County at Rural County Road four in Wounded Knee South Dakota. This includes everything above and below ground including minerals and any buildings or personal property on the land. This area is shown on a map called Wounded Knee Sacred Site and Memorial Land dated October twenty sixth two thousand twenty two. This land is part of where the Wounded Knee Massacre happened on December twenty ninth eighteen ninety. Tribes means the Oglala Sioux Tribe and Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe from the Cheyenne River Reservation. Both tribes are part of the Great Sioux Nation and signed the Fort Laramie Treaty of eighteen sixty eight with the United States.

sections.title: Section 3  Land Held in Restricted Fee Status by the Tribes
sections.text: The Secretary must take action within three hundred sixty five days after this law is passed. The Secretary must finish all the work needed including paperwork and small fixes to land surveys and legal descriptions so the Tribes can own this land in restricted fee status. The Secretary must also properly transfer any private and city utility and service rights or agreements for this land. There are several conditions that apply to this land. The Tribal land must follow federal laws about Indian country as defined in section eleven fifty one of title eighteen of the United States Code and be protected by rules against selling the land found in section one hundred seventy seven of title twenty five of the United States Code except as stated differently in this law. The Tribal land can only be used for purposes allowed in the agreement between the Oglala Sioux Tribe and Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe dated October twenty first two thousand twenty two. The Tribal land will still have to follow any private or city restrictions easements utility agreements or other legal claims that were in place when this law was passed. According to the agreement between the Oglala Sioux Tribe and Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe dated October twenty first two thousand twenty two the Tribal land cannot be used for gambling activities under the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act.
